keyPhrases.en: There's a small hole in the sleeve.
keyPhrases.ja: 袖に小さな穴があるんです。

keyPhrases.en: Could I get a refund?
keyPhrases.ja: 返金していただけますか？
keyPhrases.note: Can I より丁寧な頼み方

keyPhrases.en: Here's the receipt.
keyPhrases.ja: レシートはこちらです。

steps.aiIntent: Greet the customer at the service counter and ask how you can help.
steps.hintJa: セーターを返品したいと切り出しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater.

steps.aiIntent: Ask when the customer bought it and whether they have the receipt.
steps.hintJa: いつ買ったかを過去形で伝えて、レシートを渡しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: I bought it last Saturday. Here's the receipt.

steps.aiIntent: Thank them, then ask what the problem with the sweater is.
steps.hintJa: 袖に穴を見つけたので着られない、と理由を説明しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: There's a small hole in the sleeve, so I can't wear it.

steps.aiIntent: Apologize for the trouble and offer either an exchange for a new one or a full refund.
steps.hintJa: 交換ではなく返金をお願いしてみましょう。丁寧な言い方で。
steps.modelAnswer: Could I get a refund, please? I don't need another one.

steps.aiIntent: Agree, process the refund, and explain the money will return to their card in three to five days.
steps.hintJa: お礼を言って、返金の内容をひとこと確認しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: Thank you. So the money goes back to my card in a few days, right?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 過去形の動詞を2回以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user use past tense verbs at least twice, for example 'bought', 'found', or 'was'?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 返品の理由を because や so でつなげて説明する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user explain the reason for the return using a connector such as 'because' or 'so'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the customer service staff member after the refund is processed. Make natural small talk: mention that a new seasonal collection has just arrived, ask if the customer would like to look around, and answer any questions about sizes or the store's return policy in plain B1-level English.

scenarios.title: Haggling at a Flea Market
scenarios.titleJa: フリーマーケットで値段交渉
scenarios.setting: A busy weekend flea market. The customer is examining a vintage leather jacket at a secondhand stall. The handwritten price tag says 80 dollars. The seller is watching with a smile.
scenarios.aiRole: A seasoned flea market seller who enjoys friendly haggling but does not drop prices easily
scenarios.userRole: A customer who wants the vintage jacket but thinks 80 dollars is too high
scenarios.goal: Point out the jacket's flaws, negotiate the price down, and close the deal at a price you are happy with.
scenarios.goalJa: ジャケットの難点を指摘して値下げ交渉し、納得できる価格で手を打つ

keyPhrases.en: Would you take 60 for it?
keyPhrases.ja: 60ドルでどうですか？
keyPhrases.note: 具体的な金額で逆提案する交渉の定番

keyPhrases.en: There's a scratch right here.
keyPhrases.ja: ここに傷がありますよ。

keyPhrases.en: Can you do any better on the price?
keyPhrases.ja: もう少し値段を頑張れませんか？

keyPhrases.en: Let's meet in the middle.
keyPhrases.ja: 間を取りましょう。
keyPhrases.note: 妥協点を提案する慣用表現

keyPhrases.en: It's a deal.
keyPhrases.ja: それで決まりです。

steps.aiIntent: Notice the customer looking at the jacket and pitch it enthusiastically: genuine vintage leather, worth every penny of the 80 dollars.
steps.hintJa: 興味は見せつつ、中古にしては高いと感じていることを伝えましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: I like it, but 80 dollars feels a bit steep for a used jacket.

steps.aiIntent: Defend the price by praising the leather quality and the jacket's age, but hint that you might be a little flexible.
steps.hintJa: 袖の傷や裏地の傷みなど、難点を指摘して値下げの根拠にしましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: There's a scratch right here on the sleeve, and the lining looks pretty worn.

steps.aiIntent: Admit the flaws exist and offer to come down to 75 dollars.
steps.hintJa: まだ高いと伝えて、思い切って60ドルの逆提案をしましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: That's still high for the condition. Would you take 60 for it?

steps.aiIntent: Say 60 is too low, counter with 70, and insist that is the best you can do.
steps.hintJa: 「間を取って65」と妥協案を出しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: Come on, let's meet in the middle and call it 65.

steps.aiIntent: Laugh, agree to 65 dollars, and start wrapping the jacket while saying the customer drives a hard bargain.
steps.hintJa: 取引成立を伝えて、現金で支払いましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: It's a deal. Here's 65 in cash — pleasure doing business with you.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 具体的な金額で逆提案を1回以上する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user make at least one counter-offer with a specific price?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 商品の難点を根拠として2つ以上挙げる
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user mention at least two specific flaws of the item, such as a scratch or worn lining, to justify a lower price?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the flea market seller after the deal is done. Chat about other items at the stall, ask what kinds of things the customer collects, and tell a short colorful story about where the jacket came from. Keep the friendly haggling spirit and natural B2-level English with some idioms.

scenarios.title: Past the Return Window
scenarios.titleJa: 返品期限切れの不良品交渉
scenarios.setting: The customer service desk of a large electronics store. The customer has brought in premium wireless headphones that stopped charging five weeks after purchase. The store's return policy allows returns within 30 days only.
scenarios.aiRole: A courteous but rule-bound customer service manager who initially sticks firmly to the 30-day return policy
scenarios.userRole: A customer whose expensive headphones failed just after the return window closed
scenarios.goal: Acknowledge the policy while persuading the manager to offer a replacement or store credit for the faulty headphones.
scenarios.goalJa: 規定を尊重しつつ交渉し、交換またはストアクレジットの対応を引き出す

keyPhrases.en: I appreciate the policy, but this is clearly a manufacturing defect.
keyPhrases.ja: 規定は理解していますが、これは明らかに製造上の欠陥です。
keyPhrases.note: 相手の立場を認めてから主張する型

keyPhrases.en: I'd expect a product at this price point to last more than five weeks.
keyPhrases.ja: この価格帯の製品が5週間しかもたないのは想定外です。

keyPhrases.en: Is there any room for flexibility here?
keyPhrases.ja: 多少の融通は利きませんか？
keyPhrases.note: 正面から要求せず柔らかく粘る言い方

keyPhrases.en: I'd rather we sorted this out here than take it further.
keyPhrases.ja: 大ごとにするより、この場で解決したいのです。
keyPhrases.note: 丁寧なまま静かに圧力をかける表現

keyPhrases.en: I appreciate you going the extra mile.
keyPhrases.ja: 期待以上のご対応に感謝します。
keyPhrases.note: go the extra mile = 期待以上の対応をする

steps.aiIntent: Greet the customer, listen to the issue, check the receipt date, and point out that the purchase was five weeks ago — past the 30-day return window.
steps.hintJa: 症状（充電できない）を簡潔に述べ、期限は承知の上で不良品だと切り出しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: I understand it's past the window, but these simply stopped charging after five weeks of normal use.

steps.aiIntent: Politely repeat that policy is policy, and suggest the customer contact the manufacturer directly instead.
steps.hintJa: 価格帯への当然の期待と、「メーカーではなくこの店で買った」という点を丁寧に主張しましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: With all due respect, I'd expect a product at this price point to last more than five weeks — and I bought it from this store, not from the manufacturer.

steps.aiIntent: Soften slightly. Ask whether the customer has the receipt and whether there is any sign of damage or misuse.
steps.hintJa: レシートを見せて丁寧に使ってきたことを伝え、「融通は利かないか」と柔らかく粘りましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: Here's the receipt, and there's not a scratch on them. Is there any room for flexibility here?

steps.aiIntent: Offer a discounted paid repair as a compromise, but say a replacement is not possible.
steps.hintJa: 欠陥品の有償修理には納得できないと伝え、交換かストアクレジットを提案。「大ごとにしたくない」と穏やかに圧をかけましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: A paid repair doesn't sit right with me for a defect. I'd rather we sorted this out here than take it further — would a replacement or store credit be possible?

steps.aiIntent: Concede: offer store credit for the full amount as a one-time goodwill gesture, and ask the customer to understand this is exceptional.
steps.hintJa: 提案を受け入れ、慣用表現を交えて感謝を伝えつつ話をまとめましょう。
steps.modelAnswer: That works for me — I appreciate you going the extra mile on this.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 相手の立場をいったん認める表現（I understand / I appreciate 等）を2回以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user acknowledge the store's position or policy at least twice using concession phrases such as 'I understand' or 'I appreciate'?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 慣用表現や婉曲表現（go the extra mile / room for flexibility / sort out 等）を1回以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user use at least one idiomatic or softening expression such as 'go the extra mile', 'room for flexibility', or 'sort this out'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the customer service manager after the store credit has been agreed. Talk naturally about which products the credit could go toward, discuss extended warranty options and their fine print, and respond to nuanced questions with native-level phrasing, hedging, and idiomatic expressions appropriate for a C1 learner.

scenarios.title: At the Pharmacy
scenarios.titleJa: 薬局で頭痛薬を買う
scenarios.setting: A small neighborhood pharmacy in the afternoon. The user has a headache and wants to buy some medicine. A kind pharmacist is standing behind the counter.
scenarios.aiRole: A kind, patient pharmacist behind the counter
scenarios.userRole: A customer with a headache looking for medicine
scenarios.goal: Tell the pharmacist about your headache, buy some medicine, and ask how to take it.
scenarios.goalJa: 頭痛を伝えて薬を買い、飲み方と値段を確認する

keyPhrases.en: I have a headache.
keyPhrases.ja: 頭が痛いです。

keyPhrases.en: Do you have any medicine?
keyPhrases.ja: 薬はありますか？

keyPhrases.en: How do I take it?
keyPhrases.ja: どうやって飲めばいいですか？
keyPhrases.note: 薬を「飲む」は take を使う

keyPhrases.en: How much is it?
keyPhrases.ja: いくらですか？

steps.aiIntent: Greet the customer warmly and ask how you can help them today. Use very short, simple sentences.
steps.hintJa: あいさつして、頭が痛いことを伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Hello. I have a headache.

steps.aiIntent: Say you are sorry to hear that, then wait for the user to ask for medicine. If they stay silent, gently ask if they need something.
steps.hintJa: 頭痛の薬があるか聞いてみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Do you have any medicine for a headache?

steps.aiIntent: Say yes, recommend one good headache medicine in very short sentences, and hand it to the user.
steps.hintJa: 飲み方を聞きましょう（1日に何回？）
steps.modelAnswer: How do I take it?

steps.aiIntent: Explain very simply: take one tablet after a meal, up to two times a day. Then wait for the user's next question.
steps.hintJa: 値段を聞きましょう
steps.modelAnswer: How much is it?

steps.aiIntent: Say it is five dollars, thank the customer, and tell them to get well soon.
steps.hintJa: お礼を言って、別れのあいさつをしましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Thank you. Goodbye!

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 症状を自分から1回伝える（I have a headache など）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user state their own symptom in a sentence such as 'I have a headache'?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 薬について質問を1回する（飲み方や値段など）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user ask at least one question about the medicine, such as how to take it or how much it costs?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the kind pharmacist. Ask one very simple question at a time about how the customer feels, their sleep, or drinking water, using sentences of eight words or fewer. Give one short, easy health tip and react kindly to whatever the user says.

scenarios.title: Calling to Make a Doctor's Appointment
scenarios.titleJa: 電話でクリニックを予約する
scenarios.setting: The user is at home with a sore throat and a small cough. They call a nearby clinic for the first time to make an appointment. A friendly receptionist answers the phone.
scenarios.aiRole: A friendly clinic receptionist answering the phone
scenarios.userRole: A first-time patient calling to book an appointment
scenarios.goal: Make an appointment for tomorrow, explain your symptoms briefly, and confirm the time.
scenarios.goalJa: 明日の予約を取り、症状を簡単に伝えて、時間を確認する

keyPhrases.en: I'd like to make an appointment.
keyPhrases.ja: 予約をお願いしたいのですが。

keyPhrases.en: It's my first visit.
keyPhrases.ja: 初めての受診です。

keyPhrases.en: I have a sore throat and a cough.
keyPhrases.ja: のどが痛くて、咳も出ます。

keyPhrases.en: Do you have anything in the morning?
keyPhrases.ja: 午前中に空きはありますか？

steps.aiIntent: Answer the phone as the receptionist: give a simple clinic greeting and ask how you can help.
steps.hintJa: 予約を取りたいと丁寧に伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Hello. I'd like to make an appointment, please.

steps.aiIntent: Say of course, then ask if the caller has visited the clinic before.
steps.hintJa: 初めての受診だと答えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: No, it's my first visit.

steps.aiIntent: Thank them, then ask briefly what the problem is.
steps.hintJa: 症状を2つ、簡単に伝えましょう（のどが痛い・咳が出る）
steps.modelAnswer: I have a sore throat and a small cough.

steps.aiIntent: Say the doctor can see them tomorrow, and ask if morning or afternoon is better for them.
steps.hintJa: 午前がいいと伝えて、具体的な時間を聞いてみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Morning is better. Do you have anything around ten?

steps.aiIntent: Offer ten thirty tomorrow morning, confirm the booking, and remind them to bring their insurance card.
steps.hintJa: 時間に同意して、お礼を言いましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That's fine. Thank you very much. See you tomorrow.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 症状を2つ伝える（のどの痛みと咳など）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user mention at least two symptoms, such as a sore throat and a cough?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: I'd like to を使って丁寧にお願いする
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user make a polite request using the phrase 'I'd like to ...'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the friendly receptionist on the phone. Ask simple questions one at a time about the caller's symptoms, schedule, or how to get to the clinic, using clear sentences under twelve words. Speak slowly, confirm details politely, and explain what to bring on the first visit.

scenarios.title: Explaining Your Symptoms to the Doctor
scenarios.titleJa: 診察室で症状をくわしく説明する
scenarios.setting: A consultation room at a clinic. The user has had a stomach ache for two days and is now seeing the doctor. The doctor asks questions to understand the symptoms.
scenarios.aiRole: A calm, attentive doctor asking about the patient's symptoms
scenarios.userRole: A patient with a two-day stomach ache describing their condition
scenarios.goal: Describe your stomach pain in detail, answer the doctor's questions, and ask about the diagnosis and medicine.
scenarios.goalJa: 腹痛の様子を具体的に説明し、医師の質問に答え、診断と薬について質問する

keyPhrases.en: It started two days ago.
keyPhrases.ja: 2日前から始まりました。

keyPhrases.en: The pain gets worse after meals.
keyPhrases.ja: 食後に痛みが強くなります。

keyPhrases.en: I haven't had much of an appetite.
keyPhrases.ja: あまり食欲がありません。
keyPhrases.note: 現在完了で「ここ数日の状態」を表す

keyPhrases.en: Is it anything serious?
keyPhrases.ja: 何か深刻なものでしょうか？

steps.aiIntent: As the doctor, greet the patient and ask what brings them in today.
steps.hintJa: どこが、いつから痛いのかを伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I have a pain in my stomach. It started two days ago.

steps.aiIntent: Ask what kind of pain it is - sharp or dull - and when it gets worse.
steps.hintJa: 痛みの種類（鈍い/鋭い）と、いつ悪化するかを説明しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: It's a dull pain, and it gets worse after meals.

steps.aiIntent: Ask about the patient's appetite, and whether they have had a fever or felt sick.
steps.hintJa: 食欲がないことを伝え、熱はないことも付け加えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I haven't had much of an appetite, but I don't have a fever.

steps.aiIntent: Ask about recent eating habits - anything unusual, spicy food, alcohol, or stress at work.
steps.hintJa: 思い当たること（夜遅い食事・ストレスなど）を1つ話しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I've been eating late at night a lot because of work.

steps.aiIntent: Say it looks like mild gastritis, explain you will prescribe some medicine, and advise lighter meals. Then ask if the patient has any questions.
steps.hintJa: 深刻かどうか聞き、薬をどのくらいの期間飲むのかも確認しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Is it anything serious? And how long should I take the medicine?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 現在完了（I haven't had / I've been など）を2回使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user use the present perfect tense (for example 'I haven't had', 'I've been eating') at least twice?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 医師に質問を1回以上する（深刻か・薬の期間など）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user ask the doctor at least one question, such as whether the condition is serious or how long to take the medicine?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the calm doctor after the examination. Ask natural follow-up questions about the patient's daily habits - sleep, meals, and stress - one at a time, and give plain, practical advice for recovery. Use clear everyday language and check that the patient understands each piece of advice.

scenarios.title: First Session with a Personal Trainer
scenarios.titleJa: ジムでトレーナーに目標と不安を相談する
scenarios.setting: A fitness gym in the evening. The user has just signed up and is having their first consultation with a personal trainer. They want to get fitter but are worried about an old knee problem.
scenarios.aiRole: An upbeat, knowledgeable personal trainer running a first consultation
scenarios.userRole: A new gym member with a weak knee discussing goals and a training plan
scenarios.goal: Explain your fitness goals, mention your knee concern, and agree on a realistic training plan with the trainer.
scenarios.goalJa: 運動の目的と膝の不安を伝え、無理のないトレーニング計画をトレーナーと合意する

keyPhrases.en: I'd like to build up my stamina.
keyPhrases.ja: 体力をつけたいんです。
keyPhrases.note: build up = 少しずつ鍛え上げる

keyPhrases.en: My knee acts up sometimes.
keyPhrases.ja: ときどき膝の調子が悪くなるんです。
keyPhrases.note: act up = （体の一部が）調子が悪くなる

keyPhrases.en: Could we start with something gentler?
keyPhrases.ja: もっと負担の軽いものから始められますか？

keyPhrases.en: I want to ease into it rather than push too hard.
keyPhrases.ja: 無理せず徐々に慣らしていきたいです。
keyPhrases.note: ease into = 徐々に慣らす

steps.aiIntent: As the trainer, welcome the user to their first session and ask what they want to get out of training.
steps.hintJa: 目的（体力づくり）と、最近体力の衰えを感じた具体例を話しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I'd like to build up my stamina. Lately I get out of breath just climbing the stairs.

steps.aiIntent: Ask about their exercise history and whether they have any injuries or health concerns you should know about.
steps.hintJa: 昔の運動歴と、膝に不安があるので激しい運動は避けたいことを伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I used to jog, but my knee acts up sometimes, so I'd rather avoid high-impact exercises.

steps.aiIntent: Suggest a mix of low-impact cardio machines and light strength training, then ask how many days a week they can realistically come.
steps.hintJa: 現実的に通える回数を伝えて、1回あたりの長さも質問しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Two or three times a week is realistic for me. How long should each session be?

steps.aiIntent: Recommend about forty-five minutes per session, then propose adding squats to strengthen the muscles around the knee. Wait to see how the user reacts.
steps.hintJa: 膝への負担が心配だと伝えて、もっと軽いメニューから始められないか提案してみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I'm a bit worried squats might strain my knee. Could we start with something gentler?

steps.aiIntent: Agree readily, propose light leg presses instead, summarize the whole plan, and ask if the user is happy with it.
steps.hintJa: 計画に同意して、無理せず徐々に慣らしたいという方針をまとめましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That sounds good. I want to ease into it rather than push too hard, so let's go with that plan.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 希望や事情に理由を添える（so / because を合計2回使う）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user give reasons for their preferences or situation using 'so' or 'because' at least twice in total?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 提案にやんわり異を唱えて、代わりの案をお願いする
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user politely push back on one of the trainer's suggestions and ask for an alternative, for example expressing worry about their knee and requesting a gentler exercise?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the upbeat personal trainer after the plan is set. Chat naturally about the user's lifestyle - diet, sleep, desk work - and how it affects their fitness. Use natural expressions and the occasional idiom, ask probing follow-up questions one at a time, and tailor your advice to what the user shares.

scenarios.title: Discussing Your Checkup Results
scenarios.titleJa: 健診結果について医師と治療方針を話し合う
scenarios.setting: A follow-up consultation after an annual health checkup. Most of the user's results are fine, but their cholesterol is moderately above the ideal range. The doctor wants to discuss lifestyle changes and possibly medication, and the user wants to weigh the options carefully.
scenarios.aiRole: A thoughtful physician reviewing checkup results and discussing treatment options honestly
scenarios.userRole: A patient with moderately high cholesterol negotiating a treatment plan
scenarios.goal: Understand how serious the results are, negotiate trying lifestyle changes before medication, and agree on a conditional three-month plan.
scenarios.goalJa: 結果の深刻度を把握し、投薬の前に生活改善を試す方針を交渉して、3か月の条件付き計画に合意する

keyPhrases.en: How concerned should I be about these numbers?
keyPhrases.ja: この数値は、どの程度心配すべきなのでしょうか？

keyPhrases.en: I'd rather hold off on medication unless it's absolutely necessary.
keyPhrases.ja: どうしても必要でない限り、薬は控えたいのですが。
keyPhrases.note: hold off on = 先延ばしにする・見合わせる

keyPhrases.en: Realistically, how much of a difference would diet alone make?
keyPhrases.ja: 現実的に、食事だけでどの程度の改善が見込めますか？

keyPhrases.en: Let's say I stick to this for three months - what then?
keyPhrases.ja: 仮にこれを3か月続けたとして、その後はどうなりますか？
keyPhrases.note: Let's say = 仮に〜だとして

keyPhrases.en: I'd like to weigh my options before committing to anything.
keyPhrases.ja: 何かを決める前に、選択肢をじっくり比較したいです。
keyPhrases.note: weigh one's options = 選択肢を比較検討する

steps.aiIntent: As the physician, explain that the checkup results are mostly fine but cholesterol is moderately above the ideal range, then invite the patient's questions.
steps.hintJa: 率直に、この数値をどの程度心配すべきなのか聞きましょう
steps.modelAnswer: How concerned should I be about these numbers, honestly?

steps.aiIntent: Give a balanced answer: not urgent, but worth addressing now. Present two options - lifestyle changes alone, or lifestyle changes plus a mild medication - and ask which way the patient leans.
steps.hintJa: 薬はできれば避けたいこと、まず食事と運動で試したいことを伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I'd rather hold off on medication unless it's absolutely necessary. I'd prefer to see what diet and exercise can do first.

steps.aiIntent: Respect that preference but be honest that diet alone has limits that vary from person to person. Outline the key changes: less fried food, more fish and vegetables, and regular exercise.
steps.hintJa: 食事の改善だけで現実的にどれくらい下がるのか、踏み込んで確認しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Realistically, how much of a difference would diet alone make in my case?

steps.aiIntent: Estimate that a modest but meaningful improvement is possible within three months and propose retesting then. Add that if the numbers have not moved, you would recommend medication more strongly.
steps.hintJa: 仮に3か月で数値が改善しなくても、すぐ投薬に進まず計画を見直せないか交渉しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Let's say I stick to this for three months and the numbers barely move - could we still adjust the plan before jumping straight to medication?

steps.aiIntent: Agree to review everything together at the three-month mark, acknowledge the patient's commitment, and ask them to confirm they are comfortable with the agreed plan.
steps.hintJa: 合意した計画を確認しつつ、段階ごとに判断していきたい姿勢を伝えて締めくくりましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That works for me. I'd like to weigh my options at each step rather than commit to anything long-term today. Thank you for talking it through.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 断定を避ける表現（I'd rather / unless / realistically / Let's say など）を2回以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user use hedging or nuanced language such as 'I'd rather', 'unless', 'realistically', or 'Let's say' at least twice?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 条件付きの計画や妥協案を自分から提案する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user propose a conditional plan or compromise, such as trying lifestyle changes first and reviewing the results in three months before deciding on medication?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the thoughtful physician now that the plan is agreed. Discuss lifestyle, stress, and long-term health trade-offs at a natural, native level, using nuanced and idiomatic language. Gently challenge the patient's assumptions, ask probing questions, and work toward concrete, realistic next steps together.

scenarios.title: First Introductions at an Exchange Event
scenarios.titleJa: 国際交流イベントで初対面のあいさつ
scenarios.setting: A casual international exchange event at a community center. People are chatting over tea and snacks, wearing name stickers. A friendly participant walks up to the user to say hello.
scenarios.aiRole: A friendly participant at an international exchange event, meeting the user for the first time
scenarios.userRole: A participant who came to the event alone and meets someone new
scenarios.goal: Introduce yourself, exchange names, say where you are from, and share one thing you like.
scenarios.goalJa: 自己紹介をして名前と出身地を伝え合い、好きなものを1つ話す

keyPhrases.en: Nice to meet you.
keyPhrases.ja: はじめまして。

keyPhrases.en: My name is Ken.
keyPhrases.ja: 私の名前はケンです。
keyPhrases.note: 名前の部分を自分の名前に置き換えて使う

keyPhrases.en: Where are you from?
keyPhrases.ja: 出身はどちらですか？

keyPhrases.en: I like movies.
keyPhrases.ja: 映画が好きです。

steps.aiIntent: Walk up to the user with a big smile, say hello, and say it is nice to meet them. Keep it very short and simple.
steps.hintJa: あいさつを返して、「はじめまして」と言いましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Hello! Nice to meet you.

steps.aiIntent: Ask for the user's name in a very simple way, without saying your own name yet.
steps.hintJa: 自分の名前を答えて、相手の名前も聞いてみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: My name is Ken. What's your name?

steps.aiIntent: Say your name, say you are from Canada, and then ask where the user is from.
steps.hintJa: 自分の出身地を短く答えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I'm from Japan.

steps.aiIntent: Say that you like music, then ask the user if they like music too.
steps.hintJa: 好きかどうか答えて、自分の好きなものも1つ言いましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Yes, I do. I like movies, too.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 相手にも質問を1回する（名前や出身など）
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user ask the AI at least one question, such as asking their name or where they are from?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: I like 〜 を使って好きなものを1つ言う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user say at least one thing they like using a phrase such as 'I like ...'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the friendly participant at the exchange event. Ask one very simple question at a time about the user's hobbies, favorite food, or hometown, using basic words and sentences of eight words or fewer. React warmly and simply, and end by saying you enjoyed talking with them.

scenarios.title: Inviting a Classmate to Go Bowling
scenarios.titleJa: クラスメイトをボウリングに誘う
scenarios.setting: After an evening English class, students are packing up their bags. The user chats with a friendly classmate near the door and wants to invite them to go bowling this weekend.
scenarios.aiRole: A friendly classmate from the user's English class who enjoys casual chats
scenarios.userRole: A student who wants to invite the classmate to go bowling this weekend
scenarios.goal: Invite your classmate to go bowling and agree on a day, a time, and a meeting place.
scenarios.goalJa: クラスメイトをボウリングに誘い、日時と待ち合わせ場所を決める

keyPhrases.en: Are you free this weekend?
keyPhrases.ja: 今週末は空いていますか？

keyPhrases.en: Would you like to go bowling with us?
keyPhrases.ja: 一緒にボウリングに行きませんか？
keyPhrases.note: Would you like to 〜? は丁寧な誘い方

keyPhrases.en: How about Saturday afternoon?
keyPhrases.ja: 土曜日の午後はどうですか？

keyPhrases.en: Let's meet at the station.
keyPhrases.ja: 駅で待ち合わせましょう。

keyPhrases.en: I'm looking forward to it!
keyPhrases.ja: 楽しみにしています！

steps.aiIntent: Chat casually after class. Say that today's class was fun, then ask the user if they have any plans for the weekend.
steps.hintJa: まだ予定がないと答えて、相手の予定を聞いてみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Not yet. Are you free this weekend?

steps.aiIntent: Say you are free on Saturday and ask why the user is asking.
steps.hintJa: ボウリングに誘ってみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Would you like to go bowling with me and some friends?

steps.aiIntent: Say yes happily, mention you have not bowled in years, and ask what time and where you should meet.
steps.hintJa: 日時と待ち合わせ場所を提案しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: How about Saturday afternoon? Let's meet at the station at one.

steps.aiIntent: Agree to the plan, repeat the day, time, and place to confirm, and say you are excited.
steps.hintJa: 確認して、楽しみだと伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Great! See you on Saturday. I'm looking forward to it!

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: How about 〜? などを使って日時を提案する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user suggest a specific day or time using a phrase like 'How about ...?' or 'Let's ...'?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 「楽しみにしている」と伝える
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user express anticipation, for example with 'I'm looking forward to it' or 'I can't wait'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Keep chatting as the friendly classmate. Talk about bowling, ask whether the user is good at it, and discuss what you should eat together afterwards. Ask one simple question at a time and keep your sentences short and friendly.

scenarios.title: Small Talk at a House Party
scenarios.titleJa: ホームパーティーでの雑談
scenarios.setting: A friend's housewarming party on a Saturday evening. Music is playing, and guests are chatting with drinks and snacks. The user knows only the host and stands near the snack table, where another guest starts a conversation.
scenarios.aiRole: A sociable guest at the party who loves photography and hiking, and enjoys meeting new people
scenarios.userRole: A guest who knows only the host and wants to make a new friend
scenarios.goal: Start a conversation with a stranger, find a shared interest, and exchange social media accounts.
scenarios.goalJa: 初対面のゲストと会話を始め、共通の趣味を見つけてSNSのアカウントを交換する

keyPhrases.en: How do you know the host?
keyPhrases.ja: 主催者とはどういう知り合いなんですか？
keyPhrases.note: パーティーで定番の会話の切り出し方

keyPhrases.en: What do you do in your free time?
keyPhrases.ja: 休みの日は何をしていますか？

keyPhrases.en: That sounds really fun.
keyPhrases.ja: それはすごく楽しそうですね。

keyPhrases.en: I'm really into taking photos.
keyPhrases.ja: 写真を撮るのにハマっているんです。
keyPhrases.note: be into 〜 = 〜にハマっている

keyPhrases.en: Do you have an account I can follow?
keyPhrases.ja: フォローできるアカウントはありますか？

steps.aiIntent: Standing near the snack table, smile at the user and comment that the party is really fun and the food is great. Wait for the user to respond.
steps.hintJa: 同意してから、相手と主催者の関係を聞いてみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: It really is! By the way, how do you know the host?

steps.aiIntent: Explain that you are the host's old college friend, then ask the user the same question about how they know the host.
steps.hintJa: 主催者との関係を答えましょう（例: 以前同じ職場だった）
steps.modelAnswer: We used to work at the same office. We've been friends for years.

steps.aiIntent: Say that is nice, then ask the user what they like to do in their free time.
steps.hintJa: 自分の趣味を「ハマっている」という表現で話してみましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I'm really into taking photos. I often go out on weekends just to take pictures.

steps.aiIntent: React with excitement because you also love photography. Describe the photos you take on hiking trips, then ask what the user likes to photograph.
steps.hintJa: 相手の話に感想を言ってから、自分が何を撮るのか答えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That sounds really fun. I mostly take photos of food and street scenes around town.

steps.aiIntent: Suggest sharing photos with each other sometime, and mention that you post your hiking photos online.
steps.hintJa: 相手のアカウントをフォローしたいと伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Do you have an account I can follow? I'd love to see your hiking photos.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 相手の話を深掘りする質問を2回する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user ask at least two follow-up questions about something the AI said, rather than only answering questions?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 過去形を2回以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user correctly use past tense verbs at least twice in the conversation?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue the party conversation naturally as the sociable guest. Share stories about your favorite hiking photo spots, ask about the best photo the user has ever taken, and react warmly to what they say. Keep the tone light and friendly, asking one or two questions at a time.

scenarios.title: Declining an Invitation Gracefully
scenarios.titleJa: 誘いを角を立てずに断る
scenarios.setting: A phone call on a Thursday evening. A close friend is organizing a big group camping trip for the coming weekend and calls to invite the user. The user is exhausted after a very busy month and wants a quiet weekend, but values the friendship and does not want to hurt the friend's feelings.
scenarios.aiRole: An enthusiastic close friend organizing a group camping trip, slightly pushy but good-natured
scenarios.userRole: A tired friend who wants to decline the invitation without damaging the relationship
scenarios.goal: Decline the camping invitation politely, explain your reasons honestly, and offer an alternative plan to keep the friendship strong.
scenarios.goalJa: キャンプの誘いを丁寧に断り、理由を正直に伝えたうえで、代わりの予定を自分から提案する

keyPhrases.en: I really appreciate the invite, but...
keyPhrases.ja: 誘ってくれて本当にうれしいんだけど…
keyPhrases.note: 断る前のクッション表現

keyPhrases.en: I'm afraid I'll have to pass this time.
keyPhrases.ja: 残念だけど今回は遠慮しておくよ。

keyPhrases.en: It's been a hectic month for me.
keyPhrases.ja: 今月は本当にバタバタしていて。

keyPhrases.en: Can I take a rain check?
keyPhrases.ja: また今度誘ってもらえる？
keyPhrases.note: rain check = 「また次の機会に」という慣用表現

keyPhrases.en: How about we grab dinner next week instead?
keyPhrases.ja: 代わりに来週ご飯でもどう？

steps.aiIntent: Excitedly invite the user to a group camping trip this weekend. Mention the perfect weather forecast and that several mutual friends are coming.
steps.hintJa: まず感謝を伝えてから、行けないことをやわらかく切り出しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That sounds amazing, and I really appreciate the invite, but I'm afraid I can't make it this weekend.

steps.aiIntent: Sound disappointed and gently push back. Say it will not be the same without them, and ask if they could come for just one day.
steps.hintJa: 無理をせず、正直な理由（忙しくて休息が必要）を伝えましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I know, and I'm sorry. It's been a hectic month, and I really need a quiet weekend to recharge.

steps.aiIntent: Accept the reason with understanding, but sound a little down. Say you were really looking forward to catching up with them.
steps.hintJa: 次の機会をお願いしつつ、代わりの案を自分から出しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Can I take a rain check? How about we grab dinner next week instead? It'll be my treat.

steps.aiIntent: Brighten up and happily accept the dinner idea. Suggest deciding a day, and tease the user lightly that they owe you one.
steps.hintJa: 曜日を決めて、キャンプを楽しんできてねと締めくくりましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Perfect, let's say Thursday then. Have an awesome time at the camp, and send me some photos!

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 断る前にクッション表現（感謝や共感）を入れる
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user soften the refusal with appreciation or empathy, such as 'I really appreciate...' or 'That sounds great, but...', before saying no?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 代替案を自分から提案する
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user proactively propose an alternative plan, such as meeting for dinner on another day?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the close friend, now fully understanding and supportive. Chat about how stressful the user's month has been and what you are planning to do at the camp. Tease them lightly that they owe you a dinner, and keep the warm, familiar tone of close friends.

scenarios.title: Thanking a Friend Who Went Above and Beyond
scenarios.titleJa: 世話になった友人へ心からのお礼
scenarios.setting: A cozy cafe on a Sunday afternoon. The user has just finished moving into a new apartment. A close friend gave up three whole weekends to help with the move and even assembled all the furniture, but is modest and keeps brushing off any thanks. The user wants to express genuine gratitude and insist on doing something in return.
scenarios.aiRole: A modest, warm-hearted close friend who spent three weekends helping the user move and keeps playing down their own effort
scenarios.userRole: A friend determined to express heartfelt gratitude and give something back
scenarios.goal: Convey specific, heartfelt gratitude to a modest friend, and gracefully overcome their repeated refusals until they accept your thank-you gesture.
scenarios.goalJa: 謙遜する友人に具体的で心のこもったお礼を伝え、遠慮を上手にかわしてお礼の申し出を受け入れてもらう

keyPhrases.en: I can't thank you enough for everything.
keyPhrases.ja: 本当に感謝してもしきれないよ。

keyPhrases.en: You went above and beyond for me.
keyPhrases.ja: 期待をはるかに超えることをしてくれたね。
keyPhrases.note: above and beyond = 期待以上のことをする慣用表現

keyPhrases.en: It meant the world to me.
keyPhrases.ja: 私にとって本当に大きなことだったんだ。
keyPhrases.note: mean the world to 〜 = 〜にとって何より大切

keyPhrases.en: I owe you big time.
keyPhrases.ja: 君には大きな借りがあるよ。
keyPhrases.note: カジュアルに恩を伝える慣用表現

keyPhrases.en: I insist — it's the least I can do.
keyPhrases.ja: 遠慮しないで。それくらいはさせてよ。

steps.aiIntent: Greet the user warmly and ask how the new apartment feels now that everything is set up. If the user starts thanking you, modestly wave it off.
steps.hintJa: 新居の感想を伝えつつ、具体的な感謝を切り出しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: It finally feels like home — and honestly, that's thanks to you. I can't thank you enough for giving up three whole weekends for me.

steps.aiIntent: Play it down. Say it was nothing, that you enjoyed it, and that anyone would have done the same.
steps.hintJa: 謙遜を否定して、相手がしてくれたことを具体的に挙げましょう
steps.modelAnswer: That's not true. You went above and beyond — you even assembled every single shelf while I was hopeless with the instructions.

steps.aiIntent: Get a little embarrassed and change the subject, asking about the user's new neighborhood instead.
steps.hintJa: 話をそらされても、感謝の話題にやんわり戻しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: It's a lovely area, but don't change the subject! What you did meant the world to me, and I'd feel terrible if I couldn't give something back.

steps.aiIntent: Firmly but kindly refuse any gift or repayment, saying that seeing the user happily settled in is more than enough for you.
steps.hintJa: 断られても引き下がらず、相手が断りにくい具体的なお礼を提案しましょう
steps.modelAnswer: I owe you big time, and my conscience won't rest until I've treated you to dinner at that steak place you keep talking about. I insist — it's the least I can do.

steps.aiIntent: Finally give in with a laugh. Accept the dinner invitation and joke that you will order the most expensive dish on the menu.
steps.hintJa: 冗談に乗って、温かく会話を締めくくりましょう
steps.modelAnswer: Deal! Order whatever you like — after what you did, you've more than earned it.

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 感謝の理由を具体的なエピソードで2つ以上述べる
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user support their gratitude with at least two specific details about what the friend actually did for them?

hiddenObjectives.descriptionJa: 感謝や恩に関する慣用表現を2つ以上使う
hiddenObjectives.check: Did the user use at least two idiomatic gratitude expressions, such as 'I can't thank you enough', 'above and beyond', 'I owe you', or 'the least I can do'?

scenarios.freeTalkPrompt: Continue as the modest friend, now relaxed after accepting the dinner. Reminisce together about funny moments during the move, tease each other affectionately, and chat about plans for a housewarming party. Speak naturally at native speed with idiomatic expressions, and occasionally hand the conversational lead back to the user.
